title: Atal Pension Yojana
meta_description: Atal Pension Yojana gives guaranteed monthly pension of ₹1,000-5,000 after age 60. Check contribution chart, eligibility, and how to enroll.

hero.one_liner: Guaranteed monthly pension of ₹1,000 to ₹5,000 after age 60 for workers in unorganized sector

what_is_it: Atal Pension Yojana (APY) is a government-backed pension scheme launched on 9 May 2015, targeting the unorganized sector workers who don't have any formal retirement savings. It provides a guaranteed fixed monthly pension of ₹1,000, ₹2,000, ₹3,000, ₹4,000, or ₹5,000 after the subscriber turns 60 years old.

The contribution amount depends on the age at which you join and the pension amount you choose. The younger you join, the less you pay per month. For example, if you join at age 18 and choose ₹5,000/month pension, you pay only ₹210/month. If you join at age 35, you'd pay ₹902/month for the same pension. After the subscriber's death, the spouse receives the same pension amount. After both pass away, the nominee gets the corpus amount.

eligibility.label: Age
eligibility.value: Must be between 18-40 years to join

eligibility.label: Bank account
eligibility.value: Must have a savings bank account with any bank

eligibility.label: Aadhaar & mobile
eligibility.value: Must have Aadhaar and mobile number linked to bank account

eligibility.label: Who cannot join
eligibility.value: Income taxpayers (those who file ITR) are not eligible since Oct 2022

eligibility.label: Existing pension
eligibility.value: Must not be a member of any other statutory social security scheme

how_to_apply.title: Visit your bank branch
how_to_apply.description: Go to the bank where you have a savings account. Ask for the APY enrollment form. You can also apply through net banking or mobile banking apps of most banks.

how_to_apply.title: Choose your pension amount
how_to_apply.description: Select the monthly pension you want: ₹1,000, ₹2,000, ₹3,000, ₹4,000, or ₹5,000. Higher pension = higher monthly contribution.

how_to_apply.title: Auto-debit setup
how_to_apply.description: Your monthly contribution will be auto-debited from your bank account. Ensure sufficient balance on the deduction date to avoid penalties.

how_to_apply.title: Get PRAN (Permanent Retirement Account Number)
how_to_apply.description: After enrollment, you receive a PRAN. Use this to track your account, check statements, and manage your APY account online.

important_alert.text: If auto-debit fails due to insufficient balance, a penalty of ₹1-10 per month is charged depending on the contribution amount. After 6 consecutive defaults, the account is frozen. After 12 defaults, it's closed and only the accumulated amount is returned.

faqs.question: How much do I need to pay per month?
faqs.answer: It depends on your age and chosen pension. Example for ₹5,000/month pension: Join at 18 → ₹210/month. Join at 25 → ₹376/month. Join at 30 → ₹577/month. Join at 35 → ₹902/month. Join at 40 → ₹1,454/month.

faqs.question: Can I withdraw before 60?
faqs.answer: Premature exit is generally not allowed except in case of death or terminal illness. If you exit, you only get back your contributions plus actual returns (not the guaranteed pension).

faqs.question: What happens after I die?
faqs.answer: Your spouse receives the same monthly pension for their lifetime. After both spouse and subscriber pass away, the accumulated corpus (₹1.7L to ₹8.5L depending on pension chosen) is paid to the nominee.

faqs.question: Can salaried people with EPF join APY?
faqs.answer: Yes, if you're not an income taxpayer. Having EPF doesn't disqualify you. But if you file ITR, you're not eligible since October 2022.

faqs.question: Is APY contribution tax-deductible?
faqs.answer: Yes. APY contributions qualify for Section 80CCD(1B) deduction up to ₹50,000, which is over and above the ₹1.5 lakh limit of Section 80C.
